cards.title: Anthropic Secures California Government Partnership
cards.body: Anthropic has forged a deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price, strengthening the company's relationship with the state. The partnership positions Anthropic as a key AI provider for government operations.

cards.title: South Korea Invests $550B in AI Infrastructure
cards.body: South Korean memory chip giants are committing over $550 billion to build new memory lab fabs to address supply constraints. The investment positions South Korea as a major AI tech powerhouse.

cards.title: Arena AI Leaderboard Reaches $100M Valuation
cards.body: Arena, the popular free AI leaderboard platform, has grown into a $100 million business after launching commercial services. The platform has become the industry standard for evaluating AI model performance.

cards.title: Cursor Launches Mobile App for Coding Agents
cards.body: Cursor has released a new mobile app enabling remote oversight of coding agents on the go. The tool allows developers to guide AI coding agents from anywhere.

cards.title: TIDAL Blocks AI Music Monetization
cards.body: TIDAL has implemented a new policy preventing AI-generated music from earning money on its service. The move reflects growing industry concerns about AI-created content.

cards.title: Proception Raises $11M After Tesla Settlement
cards.body: Robotics startup Proception settled a Tesla trade secret suit and announced an $11 million funding round. The company specializes in collecting training data for robot hands.

cards.title: Omen AI Raises $31M for Data Center Optimization
cards.body: Omen AI secured $31 million in Series A funding to monitor chip coolant and prevent bacterial outbreaks in data centers. The startup addresses critical infrastructure challenges in AI compute.

cards.title: Ford Rehires Engineers as AI Falls Short
cards.body: Ford has rehired experienced engineers after discovering that AI alone cannot produce high-quality products. The company acknowledged that artificial intelligence requires human expertise to succeed.

cards.title: Wall Street Eyes Micron as Next Nvidia
cards.body: Wall Street investors believe memory chip maker Micron could become the next major AI beneficiary like Nvidia. The company is positioned to capitalize on growing AI infrastructure demand.

cards.title: Skepticism Grows Over Orbital Data Centers
cards.body: SoftBank's CEO and others question Elon Musk's vision for orbital data centers. Industry leaders remain unconvinced of the feasibility and necessity of space-based compute.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro Exec Joins OpenAI
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's Vice President overseeing Vision Pro, is leaving to join OpenAI's hardware team. The move signals OpenAI's expanded focus on hardware development.

cards.title: Founder Uses Claude to Fight Cancer
cards.body: A startup founder fed his medical data—blood results, scans, wearables, and journals—into Claude to develop a personalized cancer treatment strategy. The approach demonstrates AI's emerging role in healthcare.

cards.title: Asian Startups Launch Models Amid Export Ban
cards.body: Asian AI startups are launching new models with Mythos-like capabilities to circumvent Anthropic's export restrictions. The trend could reshape the global AI market dynamics.

cards.title: Trump Administration Authorizes Mythos 5 Access
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies are authorized to use Anthropic's Mythos 5 model. The approval includes access for non-American employees within these organizations.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout After Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6's rollout following a government request, expressing concern that such processes shouldn't become standard. The company argues that government controls limit access for developers and global partners.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI recruited Uber India's chief to lead its operations in one of its largest markets outside the U.S. The hire marks the company's major push into India with expanded offices and partnerships.

cards.title: OpenAI Unveils Jalapeño Custom Inference Chip
cards.body: OpenAI revealed Jalapeño, its custom inference chip built with Broadcom, joining Google, Apple, and SpaceX in reducing Nvidia dependence. The move signals a major shift in the AI chip landscape.

cards.title: AI Competition Now Has Political Consequences
cards.body: AI model capabilities have evolved to the point where they carry real political ramifications. Industry progress requires collective action rather than individual company competition.

cards.title: Anthropic Strikes California Government AI Deal
cards.body: Anthropic has partnered with California to provide Claude AI at half price for state government use. This deal strengthens Anthropic's position against OpenAI rivals.

cards.title: Arena AI Leaderboard Reaches $100M Valuation
cards.body: The popular free AI model leaderboard has become a $100M business after launching commercial services in September. Arena helps developers compare and benchmark AI models.

cards.title: Cursor Launches Mobile App for Coding Agents
cards.body: Cursor released a new mobile app for remote oversight of AI coding agents on the go. The tool enables developers to guide their coding assistants from anywhere.

cards.title: TIDAL Blocks AI-Generated Music Monetization
cards.body: TIDAL implemented a new policy preventing AI-generated music from earning revenue on its platform. This move protects human artists from AI music competition.

cards.title: Omen AI Secures $31M to Optimize Data Centers
cards.body: Omen AI raised Series A funding to monitor chip coolant and prevent bacterial outbreaks in AI data centers. The startup's AI solution tackles critical infrastructure challenges.

cards.title: Ford Rehires Engineers After AI Shortcomings
cards.body: Ford admitted that relying solely on AI proved insufficient for producing high-quality vehicles. The company rehired experienced engineers to work alongside AI systems.

cards.title: Apple Vision Pro Exec Joins OpenAI Hardware Team
cards.body: Paul Meade, Apple's VP of Vision Pro, is leaving to join OpenAI's hardware division. The move signals OpenAI's expanding focus on AI hardware development.

cards.title: Founder Uses Claude AI to Fight Cancer
cards.body: Conno Christou leveraged Claude to analyze his medical data, scan results, and wearable outputs in his cancer battle. The AI helped synthesize complex health information into actionable insights.

cards.title: Asian AI Startups Launch Mythos Alternatives
cards.body: New AI models are launching across Asia with Mythos-like capabilities to bypass Anthropic's export restrictions. This shift threatens U.S. AI labs' dominance in Asian markets.

cards.title: Trump Administration Authorizes Mythos 5 Usage
cards.body: Over 100 U.S. companies and government agencies gained authorization to use Anthropic's Mythos 5 model. The approval includes non-American employees of authorized organizations.

cards.title: OpenAI Limits GPT-5.6 Rollout After Government Request
cards.body: OpenAI restricted GPT-5.6's release following government restrictions, criticizing mandatory access processes. The company warned such requirements limit tools for developers and cyber defenders.

cards.title: OpenAI Hires Uber India Chief for Expansion
cards.body: OpenAI recruited Uber India's chief to lead operations in its largest market outside the U.S. The hire accelerates OpenAI's expansion in India through offices and partnerships.

cards.title: Tech Giants Build Custom AI Chips vs Nvidia
cards.body: OpenAI, Google, Apple, and SpaceX are developing proprietary AI chips to reduce Nvidia dependence. OpenAI's custom Jalapeño inference chip signals a shift away from total GPU reliance.

cards.title: OpenAI's Jalapeño Chip Challenges Nvidia Dominance
cards.body: OpenAI unveiled Jalapeño, a custom inference chip built with Broadcom to reduce Nvidia dependency. The move reflects growing tech industry efforts to establish AI chip alternatives.

cards.title: AI Models Now Have Real Political Consequences
cards.body: As AI capabilities mature, their political impact demands collective action beyond individual company concerns. The Anthropic vs. OpenAI narrative has evolved into broader governance questions.

cards.title: AI Agents Aren't Your Coworkers, Expert Warns
cards.body: MIT Technology Review cautions against treating AI agents as actual workplace colleagues despite company marketing. The distinction matters for understanding AI's actual role in enterprises.

cards.title: Enterprise AI Investment Hits Inflection Point
cards.body: Gartner identifies 2026 as an inflection year for organizations to align AI projects with business goals. Companies increasingly pressure AI systems to prove measurable financial returns.

cards.title: AI Reshapes Retail Behind the Scenes
cards.body: Artificial intelligence is transforming retail decision-making in search algorithms, inventory management, and pricing rather than flashy consumer-facing features. These backend AI applications have broader impact than visible chatbots.

cards.title: IBM Chip Technology Extends Moore's Law
cards.body: IBM unveiled a prototype chip with 100 billion transistors at unprecedented density, doubling previous records. The breakthrough could power faster, more efficient AI systems for years.

cards.title: NYT Sues Microsoft Over OpenAI Supercomputer
cards.body: The New York Times accused Microsoft of building a copyright-infringing supercomputer for OpenAI. The legal challenge targets the infrastructure supporting large language model training.
